title: Allergic reactions
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Allergic reactions can include rash, itching, swelling, or breathing symptoms.
Anaphylaxis is a sudden severe allergic reaction that needs emergency treatment.
Danger signs include trouble breathing, throat swelling, hoarse voice, wheezing, or severe systemic symptoms.
Patients with serious allergic reactions may need epinephrine and follow-up planning.
